[T035B_final_result_revealed] FINAL: Presidential Runoff — Erdoğan Wins 52.18% vs Kılıçdaroğlu 47.82%: As a Young Urban Protest Voter, I react to FINAL: Presidential Runoff — Erdoğan Wins 52.18% vs Kılıçdaroğlu 47.82% through my existing identity, media exposure, and lived pressures.

[T001] 2018 Presidential & Parliamentary Elections — Erdoğan Wins First Round: I'm feeling really frustrated and angry after Erdoğan's win in the first round. It feels like the democratic process is being undermined, especially with the new presidential system concentrating so much power in one person. I worry about what this means for the future of our country and the opposition.

[T002] Turkish Lira Currency Crisis — US-Turkey Brunson Sanctions: I'm feeling really angry and frustrated about the economic situation right now. The currency crisis is hitting us hard, and it feels like the government is not doing enough to address it. I worry about how this will affect my future and the future of my peers.

[T003] ECHR Orders Demirtaş Release — Turkey Refuses: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration about the government's refusal to comply with the ECHR ruling for Demirtaş's release. It feels like our institutions are failing us, and this only deepens my distrust in the government.

[T004] March 2019 Local Elections — AKP Loses Ankara and Istanbul: I'm feeling a mix of anger and hope after the local elections. The AKP's losses in Ankara and Istanbul show that change is possible, but I still worry about the government's response and the legitimacy of the results.

[T005] YSK Annuls Istanbul Election — Rerun Ordered, İmamoğlu Wins by Landslide: I'm feeling a mix of anger and hope after İmamoğlu's landslide win. It shows that we can challenge the government's authority, but I remain worried about the implications of the YSK's annulment.

[T006] Emine Bulut Femicide — '#OlmekIstemiyorum' Goes Viral: The brutal murder of Emine Bulut has shaken me to my core. I feel a deep sense of anger and sadness as it highlights the government's failure to protect women in our society. This tragedy is a rallying cry for change, and I hope it mobilizes more people to demand justice.

[T007] COVID-19 Pandemic — Partial Lockdowns, Economic Shock: I feel overwhelmed by the government's handling of the COVID-19 pandemic. The partial lockdowns and economic shock are hitting us hard, and it feels like the government is prioritizing economic continuity over people's lives. My anger and frustration are growing as I see the lack of support for those who are struggling the most.

[T008] Turkey Withdraws from Istanbul Convention on Women's Rights: I'm feeling a deep sense of anger and sadness about the government's decision to withdraw from the Istanbul Convention. It feels like a direct attack on women's rights and safety, and it's frustrating to see the government prioritize 'family values' over the protection of vulnerable individuals.

[T009] HDP Closure Case Filed — Party Under Existential Legal Threat: I'm feeling a mix of anger and sadness about the closure case against HDP. It feels like an attack on democracy and the rights of Kurdish people, which is deeply concerning to me.

[T010] Lira Free-Fall — 60% Devaluation in 2021 After Central Bank Rate Cuts: I'm feeling a deep sense of anger and frustration about the economic situation. The free-fall of the lira and the government's handling of it is making life incredibly difficult for people like me, especially as a student with limited income.

[T011] Parliament Rejects Pension Hike to Minimum Wage — Pensioner Anger: I'm feeling even more anger and frustration after hearing about the parliament rejecting the pension hike. It's infuriating to see the government neglecting the needs of the most vulnerable, especially when so many retirees are struggling to get by.

[T012] Inflation Peaks at 80%+ — Worst Cost-of-Living Crisis in 24 Years: I'm feeling incredibly angry and frustrated about the government's neglect of the pensioners and the worsening economic situation. The rejection of the pension hike feels like a betrayal to those who have worked hard their entire lives.

It feels like the democratic process is being undermined, especially with the new presidential system concentrating so much power in one person. I worry about what this means for the future of our country and the opposition.

I feel a deep sense of anger and sadness as it highlights the government's failure to protect women in our society. This tragedy is a rallying cry for change, and I hope it mobilizes more people to demand justice.

The partial lockdowns and economic shock are hitting us hard, and it feels like the government is prioritizing economic continuity over people's lives. My anger and frustration are growing as I see the lack of support for those who are struggling the most.

[T008] Turkey Withdraws from Istanbul Convention on Women's Rights: I'm feeling a deep sense of anger and sadness about the government's decision to withdraw from the Istanbul Convention. It feels like a direct attack on women's rights and safety, and it's frustrating to see the government prioritize 'family values' over the protection of vulnerable individuals.

[T009] HDP Closure Case Filed — Party Under Existential Legal Threat: I'm feeling a mix of anger and sadness about the closure case against HDP. It feels like an attack on democracy and the rights of Kurdish people, which is deeply concerning to me.

[T010] Lira Free-Fall — 60% Devaluation in 2021 After Central Bank Rate Cuts: I'm feeling a deep sense of anger and frustration about the economic situation. The free-fall of the lira and the government's handling of it is making life incredibly difficult for people like me, especially as a student with limited income.

[T011] Parliament Rejects Pension Hike to Minimum Wage — Pensioner Anger: I'm feeling even more anger and frustration after hearing about the parliament rejecting the pension hike. It's infuriating to see the government neglecting the needs of the most vulnerable, especially when so many retirees are struggling to get by.

[T012] Inflation Peaks at 80%+ — Worst Cost-of-Living Crisis in 24 Years: I'm feeling incredibly angry and frustrated about the government's neglect of the pensioners and the worsening economic situation. The rejection of the pension hike feels like a betrayal to those who have worked hard their entire lives.

[T013] İmamoğlu Sentenced to 2.5 Years — Opposition Rallies Around Him: I'm feeling a mix of anger and sadness about İmamoğlu's sentencing. It feels like the government is trying to silence opposition voices, and it's frustrating to see such blatant injustice. I hope this mobilizes more people to stand up against the current regime.

[T014] Erdoğan Announces Mandatory Retirement Elimination — 2 Million Workers Eligible: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration about Erdoğan's recent announcement. While some workers may benefit from the retirement changes, it feels like a desperate attempt to buy votes before the elections. The government's actions seem more about maintaining power than genuinely helping people.

[T015] Constitutional Court Freezes HDP Treasury Aid: I'm really upset about the Constitutional Court freezing HDP's treasury aid. It feels like a blatant attack on democratic processes and an attempt to silence opposition voices before the elections.

[T016] EQ-A: Kahramanmaraş Earthquake Day — 7.8 and 7.7 Magnitude Double Strike: I feel overwhelmed by the devastation from the earthquakes and the government's slow response. It's hard to see any hope when so many people are suffering and the political situation feels so dire.

[T017] EQ-B: AFAD Response Chaos — Rescue Teams Delayed, Deaths Mount: I'm feeling a deep sense of anger and sadness over the government's failure to respond effectively to the earthquake. It's frustrating to see so many people in need while the political situation remains stagnant.

[T018] EQ-C: Twitter/TikTok Banned During Earthquake Rescue — Public Outcry: I'm feeling a mix of anger and sadness after the government's decision to block Twitter and TikTok during the earthquake rescue efforts. It shows a blatant disregard for the people's need for communication and coordination in such a crisis.

[T019] EQ-D: Erdoğan Admits Response Problems, Visits Disaster Zone: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration after Erdoğan's admission of response problems. While it's somewhat refreshing to hear him acknowledge the issues, the damage has already been done, and the ban on social media during this crisis only adds to my disillusionment.

[T020] EQ-E: Contractors Arrested — Construction Amnesty and Building Safety Scandal: I'm feeling a deep sense of anger and frustration over the government's mishandling of the earthquake response and the corruption linked to construction amnesties. The fact that so many lives were lost due to negligence is infuriating, and I can't trust the government to take responsibility.

[T021] EQ-F: Opposition Mayors Respond — CHP Aid vs. Government Aid Narrative Battle: I'm feeling a mix of anger and hope as I see the opposition mayors stepping up in the earthquake response. It's frustrating to see the government struggle while they seem to be more effective. This narrative battle could really influence how people view the parties in the upcoming elections.

[T022] EQ-G: National Mourning, Reconstruction Promises, and Electoral Rescheduling Debate: I'm feeling frustrated and angry about the government's response to the earthquake. The opposition seems to be handling the situation better, which makes me question their effectiveness. I hope for real change in the upcoming elections.

[T023] OPP-A: Akşener Leaves Table of Six — Opposition Crisis: I'm really frustrated with the opposition's disunity, especially with Akşener leaving the Table of Six. It feels like our chance to make a change is slipping away, and I'm worried that this will only empower Erdoğan's camp. I just want a candidate who can effectively challenge the current government.

[T024] OPP-B: Opposition Reunites — Kılıçdaroğlu Nominated with İmamoğlu/Yavaş VP Formula: I'm relieved that the opposition has managed to unite around Kılıçdaroğlu, but I still have concerns about his ability to inspire voters compared to İmamoğlu. I hope this alliance can effectively challenge Erdoğan, but I feel a mix of anger and sadness about the past disunity.

[T025] Constitutional Court Lifts HDP Treasury Aid Freeze: I'm somewhat relieved that the Constitutional Court has lifted the HDP's treasury aid freeze, as it may help them in the upcoming elections. However, the ongoing closure case still worries me, and I feel a mix of anger and sadness about the political situation for Kurdish voters.

[T026] Erdoğan Announces Pension Increase to 7,500 Lira — Pre-Election Largesse: I'm really frustrated with Erdoğan's pension increase, which feels like a desperate attempt to buy votes rather than genuinely help people. The increase doesn't even keep up with inflation, and it just highlights how out of touch the government is with our struggles.

[T027] HDP/YSP Formally Endorses Kılıçdaroğlu — Kurdish Vote Behind Opposition: I'm feeling a mix of hope and frustration regarding the HDP's endorsement of Kılıçdaroğlu. While it could energize the opposition and bring in more votes, I'm worried about how Erdoğan will use this to frame the narrative against him.

[T028] Erdoğan Raises Civil Servant Salaries 45% — Five Days Before Election: I'm feeling really frustrated with Erdoğan's last-minute salary increase. It feels like a desperate attempt to buy votes rather than a genuine solution to the economic crisis we're facing. This tactic only adds to my anger and political fatigue as we approach the elections.
